description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring a rich, medium-brown wood grain finish. The front face is dominated by a large, convex glass screen bordered by a wooden bezel, with two prominent round control knobs and a row of push buttons beneath the screen. The back panel is metallic and perforated for ventilation, with a protruding section for the cathode ray tube and various control switches, labels, and a power cord. Both side panels are plain wood, each with a rectangular vent grille for additional airflow. The television sits on four small feet and has a classic mid-20th-century design, evoking a sense of nostalgia. The overall build suggests a heavy, robust construction typical of early television technology.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a convex glass screen, large control knobs, and a perforated metal back panel. The design features side vent grilles and a prominent wood grain finish.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the perforated metal back panel, protruding cathode ray tube housing, control switches, and attached power cord.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the smooth wooden surface and a rectangular vent grille, with the power cord visible on the ground.
description_view_2: Front view highlighting the convex glass screen, wooden bezel, two large control knobs, and a row of push buttons beneath the screen.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, featuring the wooden surface and a matching vent grille, with no visible controls or cords.
attribute_bezel_design: Medium-thick wooden bezel with a natural brown finish and rounded corners, matching the cabinet's wood grain.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large round knobs and a row of push buttons located on the lower front panel beneath the screen.
